mood.label: Risk-off correction
pills: Crude shock is the real boss today (it hits inflation, rupee, and earnings).
INR slipped to record lows intraday; currency stress = risk-off for equities.
Volatility is elevated; expect whipsaws and false breakouts when VIX > 20.
Rotation is real: power/energy held up while autos and banks got hit.
DIIs are absorbing selling, but that does not mean the bottom is in.

todayIn30Seconds: Risk-off correction (macro-led selloff; rotation into energy/power).
Protect capital, keep dry powder, and only add where price is holding up relative to Nifty (relative strength = falling less or rising when Nifty falls).
Crude oil spiked (Brent +9% on the day) raising inflation + deficit concerns for India.
Rupee hit a record low intraday; closed weaker at ~92.19 per USD.
Foreign investors remained heavy net sellers; DIIs were strong net buyers.
Autos and private banks led the fall; energy/power names were the bright spot.
Volatility stayed elevated (India VIX above 20 = elevated fear zone).
Do not chase rebounds in beaten-down autos/banks; wait for a clear base + improving crude/INR.
If you must deploy fresh cash, do it in tranches (3–5 parts) and prefer relative-strength sectors (energy/power; selective defensives).
For swing trading, cut position size and demand cleaner setups; VIX > 20 means normal stop-losses get hit more often.
Keep a watch on Nifty 23,556 (day’s low); lose it and downside opens toward ~23,200–23,000 (per key market commentary).
Markets are not reacting to company news; they are reacting to crude + currency + flows. Stay disciplined: defend, rotate, and only buy what is proving strength.

whatChanged.title: Nifty 50 close
whatChanged.description: 23866.85 (-1.63%) → 23639.15 (-0.95%). Second straight down day, but the day’s loss was smaller than the prior session; sentiment still fragile.

whatChanged.title: BSE Sensex close
whatChanged.description: 76863.71 (-1.72%) → 76034.42 (-1.08%). Broad risk-off continues; decline driven more by macro (oil/INR) than stock-specific news.

whatChanged.title: India VIX
whatChanged.description: 21.06 → 21.52. Still in elevated fear zone (>20). Expect higher intraday swings and stop-loss hunts.

whatChanged.title: Crude (Brent futures close)
whatChanged.description: 91.98 (+4.76%) → 100.46 (+9.22%). This is the key change. India is a large crude importer, so higher crude is a direct macro headwind.

whatChanged.title: USD/INR (spot close)
whatChanged.description: 92.04 → 92.19. Rupee weakened further; intraday record low was ~92.3575. Higher USD/INR = weaker rupee.

whatChanged.title: FII net flow (cash market)
whatChanged.description: ₹-6,267.31 Cr → ₹-7,049.87 Cr. Foreign selling intensified; DIIs increased buying, acting as counterweight. Treat DII support as a cushion, not a guarantee.

globalMacro.summary: Oil up sharply + INR weakness + persistent FII selling = macro-driven risk-off day.

regions.name: Macro
regions.note: Stronger dollar pressures INR and can worsen imported inflation (oil).

regions.name: Macro
regions.note: Higher US yields reduce risk appetite and can accelerate FII outflows.

regions.name: Macro
regions.note: High crude is a first-order negative for India: current account + inflation + earnings margins.

regions.name: Macro
regions.note: Gold dipped on the day even as geopolitics stayed tense; watch if it resumes as a hedge trade.

regions.name: Macro
regions.note: WTI confirms the oil shock is broad-based, not just one benchmark.

keyDrivers: If US yields and the dollar stay strong, EM flows (including India) typically face pressure; that can amplify FII selling.
Asia was broadly cautious; India is moving with the regional risk-off tone, but crude is the bigger India-specific driver.
European equities were also soft; the common thread is energy-price uncertainty.

indianMarket.summary: Macro-driven risk-off (oil + INR + flows) with rotation into power/energy.
highlights: Benchmarks fell again (Nifty -0.95%, Sensex -1.08%) with 12/16 major sectors in the red; Auto index fell ~3.19% while Nifty Energy rose ~1.93%. Rupee hit record low intraday; crude was up sharply.
This is a macro shock regime: when crude spikes and INR weakens, markets reprice growth and inflation risk fast. Stock-picking alpha reduces; sector selection and risk management matter more.
Treat dips as tactical, not as 'value'. Prefer relative strength (energy/power; selective defensives) and keep autos/banks on watch until crude and INR cool.

marketRegime.description: India VIX is above 20 (elevated fear). FIIs were net sellers while DIIs were net buyers. Oil up ~9% on the day; rupee hit record lows intraday. When volatility is elevated and the driver is macro (oil/FX), price moves become headline-driven and correlation increases (many stocks fall together). Operate with a 'capital protection' mindset: fewer trades, smaller size, faster risk cuts. Deploy long-term cash gradually only into proven quality or relative strength. Sizing: If you normally risk 1R per trade, cut to ~0.5–0.7R while India VIX stays >20. Keep stops hard and pre-decided (no emotional widening).

signals.label: What Works
signals.value: Relative-strength sectors (energy/power) and companies with pricing power.

signals.label: What Works
signals.value: Staggered buying (tranches) rather than lump-sum entries.

signals.label: What Works
signals.value: Hedges or partial de-risking (cash is a position).

signals.label: What Fails
signals.value: Averaging down fast in weak sectors (autos, private banks, rate-sensitive names).

signals.label: What Fails
signals.value: High leverage and tight-margin businesses when crude is surging.

sectorRotation.summary: Sector tape shows clear rotation: Energy index up ~1.93% while Auto fell ~3.19%; banks also weakened. Oil spike remains the central macro variable. This is not broad 'buy the dip'. Money is hiding in perceived beneficiaries (power/energy) and avoiding oil-sensitive cyclicals (auto) and FII-heavy segments (private banks).

sectors.name: Energy / Power
sectors.note: +1.93% | Positive day even in falling markets: rotation into power/energy linked to demand expectations + alternative fuel narrative.

sectors.name: Oil & Gas
sectors.note: +0.48% | Index held green despite crude shock; avoid refiners with margin risk, prefer integrated/beneficiaries where applicable.

sectors.name: Metals
sectors.note: +0.35% | Small positive day and relative outperformance versus Nifty over the week; still volatile—stick to leaders.

sectors.name: Pharma
sectors.note: -0.31% | Pharma held up well vs Nifty over the week (defensive rotation), even though it was mildly down today.

sectors.name: IT
sectors.note: -0.24% | IT fell less than Nifty; use this only for selective quality adds, not broad beta.

sectors.name: FMCG
sectors.note: -1.77% | Defensive did not protect today; likely valuation + risk-off derating. Keep for stability, add only on deeper corrections.

sectors.name: Consumption
sectors.note: -1.59% | Consumption index is slipping; oil-led inflation risk can compress discretionary spending.

sectors.name: Auto
sectors.note: -3.19% | Clear weakest sector: oil spike + risk aversion hit autos hard; wait for base formation.

sectors.name: Financials / Banks
sectors.note: -1.14% | Banks remain the key drag; wait for crude/INR stability and signs of FII selling exhaustion.

sectors.name: PSU Banks
sectors.note: -0.03% | PSU banks were almost flat today, but trend is still down. Trade only if risk is defined; do not treat as 'safe'.

sectors.name: Capital Goods / Infra
sectors.note: -0.49% | Infra index is milder vs autos/banks; still, avoid overexposure until macro stabilizes.

sectors.name: Defence
sectors.note: -0.34% | Defence index is down but not collapsing; treat as theme, not as a hedge. Add only on clean bases.

sectors.name: Telecom
sectors.note: -0.52% | Using Nifty MidSmall IT & Telecom as a proxy: mild down day and better-than-Nifty week, but liquidity can be patchy.

breadth.summary: Internal strength is weak but not capitulation: losers dominate, volatility elevated, and leadership is narrow (energy/power).

indicators.label: Sector Breadth
indicators.value: Weak: 12 out of 16 major sectors declined.

indicators.label: Defensives Leading?
indicators.value: No (FMCG -1.77%, Pharma -0.31%); 'defensive' leadership came mainly from energy/power today.

indicators.label: Cyclicals Weak?
indicators.value: Yes: Auto -3.19%, Banks -1.14% (rate- and oil-sensitive cyclicals weak).

indicators.label: Buying Type
indicators.value: Rotation + selective dip-buying (DIIs) rather than broad risk-on accumulation.

swingSetups.entry: ₹455–₹472 (prefer pullback toward ₹455–₹462 instead of buying at the day’s high)
swingSetups.stopLoss: ₹439 (below recent breakout area; if it breaks, thesis weakens)
swingSetups.thesis: Strong up day (+5.23%) with close near highs; indicates demand even in a falling market. Expectation of higher power/coal demand heading into early summer; coal remains a key power fuel. This is a tactical trade in a volatile tape—book partial profits faster than usual. R:R ~1:2 if entry on pullback Invalidation: If Coal India falls below ₹439 or if crude cools sharply and leadership rotates away from energy/power, do not force this trade. Sizing: Half size (macro headline risk).

swingSetups.entry: ₹382–₹391 (avoid chasing if it gaps up; look for intraday consolidation)
swingSetups.thesis: Closed higher (+2.80%) while the market fell; recently made a new 52-week high in the same session. Power demand narrative + sector rotation into power/energy. Use a trailing stop if price moves above ₹405; do not hold and hope in high VIX. R:R ~1:1.8 (depends on entry) Invalidation: Break below ₹375 or broad market panic (Nifty decisively under 23,556) reduces follow-through odds. Sizing: Half size; trail stop if it moves in your favor.

avoidList.reason: Auto index down ~3.19% and weekly tone is the weakest; crude spike directly hurts sentiment and costs.
Bank Nifty and private bank index remain weak; FII selling tends to hit these first in risk-off.
VIX > 20 means bigger swings; liquidity gaps can turn small losses into large ones.

support: 23556 (day’s low). Next support zone: 23200–23000 if 23556 breaks.

brent.pivot: 100 (psychological). Watch 90 as 'relief' level if crude cools.

usdInr.pivot: 92.00–92.35 (stress zone; sustained >92 keeps pressure on equities).

support: Brent and WTI cool meaningfully (e.g., Brent back below 90–95).
USD/INR stabilizes below ~92 and stops making new lows.
India VIX drops back below 18–20 and stays there.
FII net selling reduces materially or flips to net buying for a few sessions.
Nifty reclaims and holds above ~23,900 with better breadth.
resistance: Nifty breaks 23,556 and fails to recover quickly.
Crude spikes again toward/above recent highs (Brent > 101).
USD/INR sustains above the record-low zone and liquidity tightens.

investorPlan.stance: Defensive first, selective offense only in relative-strength pockets.
investorPlan.summary: Markets are being driven by crude + FX + flows more than earnings news. In such regimes, stock-specific fundamentals matter, but timing and sector positioning dominate outcomes in the short-to-medium term. Be boring: protect downside, keep cash, and scale into strength rather than averaging into weakness.

actions.action: Core long-term compounders (quality, cash flows): Hold / add slowly. Add only in tranches on deeper red days; do not rush. Macro (crude/INR) can keep pressure longer than expected.
Energy / Power (relative strength pocket): Tactical overweight (with discipline). Sector is acting as a shelter today; still avoid chasing and keep stops if trading.
Cyclicals (Auto, FII-owned banks/NBFCs): Underweight / wait. These are getting repriced for higher oil + risk aversion. Wait for crude to cool and charts to base.
Themes (Defence, infra/capex): Watchlist; selective adds only. Themes can hold better, but this tape is macro-led. Add only if price proves stability vs Nifty.

mutualFundView.summary: Continue SIPs as-is (this is when SIPs work best psychologically). Avoid full lumpsum today; use STP or 3–5 tranches over coming weeks while VIX stays elevated. Dry powder: Keep 20–35% of fresh cash as dry powder until oil/INR volatility cools.

recommendations.action: Accumulate slowly. Use tranches; avoid trying to catch the exact bottom in a macro shock.
SIP only; lumpsum cautious. They held up better today, but liquidity risk increases in risk-off markets; keep expectations realistic.
Prefer for new money if you want smoother volatility. Good choice when you are unsure about the regime; reduces timing risk.
Avoid adding fresh (unless you already have a defined thesis and limits). Sector funds are volatile and can underperform for long stretches in macro shocks.

dos: Do track crude (Brent/WTI) and USD/INR daily; they are driving equity risk right now.
Do keep stops and position sizing tighter while VIX > 20.
Do focus on relative strength sectors and liquid names (easier exits in volatile days).
Do stagger deployment of fresh cash (tranches / STP).
donts: Don’t average down aggressively in autos/banks just because prices are lower.
Don’t chase gap-ups in energy/power; wait for pullbacks or consolidations.
Don’t overtrade; high VIX days punish impatience.
Don’t assume DII buying alone will create a durable bottom.

news.title: Investor Bottom Line
news.summary: Macro risk is high. Keep cash, avoid averaging down weak cyclicals, and accumulate only in tranches into quality/relative strength.

news.title: Swing Trader Bottom Line
news.summary: Trade less and smaller. If trading, stick to relative strength (power/energy) with hard stops. Avoid autos/banks until volatility cools.

news.title: MF Investor Bottom Line
news.summary: SIP continues. For new lumpsum cash, use phased deployment (STP/tranches) until crude/INR stabilizes.
